_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
Amendment C376 does not establish a standalone registration regime for developers, owners, or logistics operators in the manner of an Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) scheme. Compliance is embedded in the planning permit application process, not in a separate registry.

**Permit-stage reporting (published requirements):**
- **Sustainable Design Assessment (SDA):** Required for smaller developments. Must detail how the development meets each standard and requirement in Schedule 73 to the Design and Development Overlay (DDO73). Can be integrated with a BESS assessment. Must be prepared by a suitably qualified person (typically an ESD consultant).
- **Sustainability Management Plan (SMP):** Required for larger developments (>5,000 sqm). Provides additional detail beyond the SDA, including credit achievement levels for Green Star Buildings and minimum requirement compliance narratives. Must be lodged with the planning permit application.
- **Green Factor Scorecard:** Must be submitted as part of landscape documentation for all relevant development applications where Green Factor applies (new developments and additions >1,000 sqm GFA).
- **NABERS Energy Commitment Agreement:** For non-residential buildings >5,000 sqm, a formal contractual commitment to design, build, and commission to a specified NABERS energy rating must be executed and lodged.
- **Waste Management Plan:** Must meet City of Melbourne Guidelines for Waste Management Plans and align with any applicable precinct waste management plan.

**Promised but not yet released:** No standalone C376 digital compliance registry or portal has been announced. Council has indicated it will provide technical assistance to applicants on suitable tools and design responses, but no formal pre-application registration process has been published. Gazettal documents and any associated practice notes have not yet been made publicly available as at the date of this brief.

**Data fields the workspace (or its clients) must collect:** building GFA, zone classification (Capital City Zone, Docklands Zone, or other), intended building use/typology, number of dwellings (residential), access to recycled water (confirmed with water retailer), site area for urban heat island calculations, proposed landscape area, and ESD tool outputs (BESS report, NatHERS certificate, Green Star registration number, NABERS Commitment Agreement reference).

**Scope:** Obligations apply to all land in the City of Melbourne municipality, but only for new planning applications lodged after the date of gazettal. Existing buildings are explicitly excluded.

*Source: Amendment C376: Sustainable Building Design, Participate Melbourne / City of Melbourne, 29 July 2025. https://participate.melbourne.vic.gov.au/amendment-c376; How does this affect me? | Amendment C376, Participate Melbourne, 29 July 2025. https://participate.melbourne.vic.gov.au/amendment-c376/how-does-this-affect-me*
